tldr: Three of seven live ads are within 10 days of fatigue, and the rotation is over-indexed on static product stills with near-identical 'Shop Now' framing. Expect CPM creep of 18-25% over the next 14 days unless two replacements ship this week.

benchmark.context: Luxury womenswear peers (Khaite, Toteme, The Row) typically extend creative life by mixing atelier B-roll with editorial stills. Roland Mouret's rotation is currently half the category median because every active ad leans on the same studio-on-white aesthetic.

ads.headline: The Galaxy Dress — Reimagined
ads.body: Sculpted to flatter. Engineered to last. Discover the iconic silhouette that defined a generation of red carpets.
drivers: Highest spend concentration in the account — frequency likely north of 4.2 on core 35-54 women
Hero image is the same front-facing studio shot used in two other active ads
Copy leans on 'iconic' and 'red carpet' — language repeated across 3 of 7 creatives, dulling pattern interrupt

ads.headline: Tailoring, Perfected
ads.body: Discover the SS24 edit. Sculptural silhouettes in Italian crepe and double-faced wool. Now shipping worldwide.
drivers: SS24 framing is now seasonally stale heading into pre-fall buying windows
Carousel uses 4 look-book frames with no motion or model walk — engagement decay typical at week 5+
'Sculptural silhouettes' phrasing duplicated in Ad #4 body copy

ads.headline: Dress Like You Mean It
ads.body: From boardroom to black tie. The Roland Mouret woman doesn't change for the occasion — the occasion changes for her.
drivers: Strong hook line but paired with a static product flat-lay — visual under-delivers on the copy's energy
Audience overlap with Ad #1 (same Galaxy-adjacent retargeting pool) accelerating frequency
No price anchor or scarcity cue — CTR softening in week 3 of flight

ads.headline: The Arden Dress
ads.body: A sculptural silhouette in stretch crepe. Designed in London. Made in Europe. Yours to wear forever.
drivers: Single-SKU focus is healthy but visual is a flat e-com still rather than on-body movement
'Made in Europe' is the strongest differentiator in the rotation but buried in line three
No social proof or press citation supporting the 'forever' claim

ads.headline: Wedding Guest Season
ads.body: Curated dressing for every invitation in your calendar. Midis, maxis, and tailored sets — all in stock, all shipping now.
drivers: Seasonal hook gives a built-in expiry date — useful, but window closes by mid-June
Edit-style carousel competing with Ad #2 for the same 'browse' intent audience
Generic 'all in stock' line — no specific dress named, weakens click intent

ads_compact.headline: Bridal by Roland Mouret
ads_compact.body: The atelier's bridal capsule. Made-to-measure silhouettes for the modern bride.

ads_compact.headline: As Seen on the Red Carpet
ads_compact.body: Worn by Cate, Scarlett, and a generation of women who understood the assignment.

ad_to_scale.headline: As Seen on the Red Carpet
ad_to_scale.body: Worn by Cate, Scarlett, and a generation of women who understood the assignment.
ad_to_scale.why: This is the only ad in the rotation leveraging real social proof, and it's the youngest by fatigue score. The named-celebrity hook is doing what the generic 'iconic silhouette' copy in Ad #1 is trying and failing to do. Push budget here before the rest of the account collapses on frequency.

ad_to_kill.headline: The Galaxy Dress — Reimagined
ad_to_kill.body: Sculpted to flatter. Engineered to last. Discover the iconic silhouette that defined a generation of red carpets.
ad_to_kill.why: Fatigue score of 91 with four days of runway left. It's eating the lion's share of spend while the visual and 'iconic silhouette' copy are duplicated across two other active creatives, creating audience burn. Pause it, then relaunch the Galaxy as a motion piece (see Concept 1).

concepts.hook: The dress that broke the internet, in motion.
concepts.primary_text: The Galaxy Dress was never meant to sit still. Watch the bonded crepe move, the seams hold, the silhouette do what no photograph can show. Engineered in our London atelier and cut to sculpt — this is the dress women buy once and wear for a decade. Now in three new colourways, shipping worldwide from the atelier.
concepts.visual_direction: Slow 360 walk on a model in the signature black Galaxy, shot on a sound stage with a single key light. Cut to detail macros of seam, dart, and zip pull.
concepts.fills_gap: Replaces three static Galaxy stills with a single motion asset — fixes the rotation's biggest creative monoculture.

concepts.primary_text: Each silhouette begins on a wooden form in our London atelier. Hand-pinned, hand-cut, then made in family-run mills across Italy and Portugal. The Arden takes 14 hours to construct. The Galaxy, 19. We don't release a dress until it can outlive a season — and most outlive a decade. This is what you're buying.
concepts.visual_direction: Phone-shot vertical footage of pattern cutters, fabric being walked through a mill, a tailor's chalk on crepe. Hand-held, no narration — soft piano, captioned text overlays.
concepts.fills_gap: Zero craft or provenance content currently in rotation; introduces the 'Made in Europe' line buried in Ad #4 as a hero narrative.

concepts.hook: One Roland Mouret. Six places it's been.
concepts.primary_text: She wore the Arden to a Tuesday board meeting. Then to a christening, a Mayfair dinner, a Como wedding, her sister's birthday, and a press launch in Paris. One dress. Six invitations. This is what sculptural tailoring is for — to be the answer, not the question. Discover the silhouettes designed to outlast the calendar.
concepts.visual_direction: Six frames of the same Arden Dress styled six ways — flats, blazer, heels, belt, evening clutch, daytime tote. Consistent model, varied location stills.
concepts.fills_gap: Replaces the generic 'Wedding Guest Season' edit with a single-SKU narrative that lifts AOV and gives retargeting a fresh angle.

unlocks: Recover an estimated 18-25% of CPM by retiring the duplicated Galaxy creatives before May 2
Extend average creative lifespan from 11 days toward the 22-day category median within one rotation cycle
Introduce two missing pillars — craft provenance and versatility — that the current account has zero coverage on
Free up budget currently trapped in fatigued top-of-funnel ads to scale the celebrity-proof creative

next_step.urgency: Ad #1 fatigues in 4 days. Ad #2 in 6.
next_step.headline: Let's ship the replacements before May closes.
next_step.body: 30 minutes to walk you through the three concepts, the production timeline, and what scaling 'As Seen on the Red Carpet' looks like with proper budget. If we move this week, you protect Q2 CPMs.

method_note: Scores combine creative repetition signals, visual/copy overlap, and time-in-flight inferred from the Meta Ad Library scrape; we don't see your in-platform frequency or CPM, so figures are directional.
